AssociateService.name: AssociateService
AssociateService.documentation: Adds a specific service association to an AgentSpace. It overwrites the existing association of the same service. Returns 201 Created on success.

CreateAgentSpace.name: CreateAgentSpace
CreateAgentSpace.documentation: Creates a new AgentSpace with the specified name and description. Duplicate space names are allowed.

CreateBacklogTask.name: CreateBacklogTask
CreateBacklogTask.documentation: Creates a new backlog task in the specified agent space

CreateChat.name: CreateChat
CreateChat.documentation: Creates a new chat execution in the specified agent space

CreatePrivateConnection.name: CreatePrivateConnection
CreatePrivateConnection.documentation: Creates a Private Connection to a target resource.

DeleteAgentSpace.name: DeleteAgentSpace
DeleteAgentSpace.documentation: Deletes an AgentSpace. This operation is idempotent and returns a 204 No Content response on success.

DeletePrivateConnection.name: DeletePrivateConnection
DeletePrivateConnection.documentation: Deletes a Private Connection. The deletion is asynchronous and returns DELETE_IN_PROGRESS status.

DescribePrivateConnection.name: DescribePrivateConnection
DescribePrivateConnection.documentation: Retrieves details of an existing Private Connection.

DisableOperatorApp.name: DisableOperatorApp
DisableOperatorApp.documentation: Disable the Operator App for the specified AgentSpace

DisassociateService.name: DisassociateService
DisassociateService.documentation: Deletes a specific service association from an AgentSpace. This operation is idempotent and returns a 204 No Content response on success.

EnableOperatorApp.name: EnableOperatorApp
EnableOperatorApp.documentation: Enable the Operator App to access the given AgentSpace

GetAccountUsage.name: GetAccountUsage
GetAccountUsage.documentation: Retrieves monthly account usage metrics and limits for the AWS account.

GetAgentSpace.name: GetAgentSpace
GetAgentSpace.documentation: Retrieves detailed information about a specific AgentSpace.

GetAssociation.name: GetAssociation
GetAssociation.documentation: Retrieves given associations configured for a specific AgentSpace.

GetBacklogTask.name: GetBacklogTask
GetBacklogTask.documentation: Gets a backlog task for the specified agent space and task id

GetOperatorApp.name: GetOperatorApp
GetOperatorApp.documentation: Get the full auth configuration of operator including any enabled auth flow

GetRecommendation.name: GetRecommendation
GetRecommendation.documentation: Retrieves a specific recommendation by its ID

GetService.name: GetService
GetService.documentation: Retrieves given service by it's unique identifier

ListBacklogTasks.name: ListBacklogTasks
ListBacklogTasks.documentation: Lists backlog tasks in the specified agent space with optional filtering and sorting

ListChats.name: ListChats
ListChats.documentation: Retrieves a paginated list of the user's recent chat executions

ListGoals.name: ListGoals
ListGoals.documentation: Lists goals in the specified agent space with optional filtering

ListJournalRecords.name: ListJournalRecords
ListJournalRecords.documentation: List journal records for a specific execution

ListPendingMessages.name: ListPendingMessages
ListPendingMessages.documentation: List pending messages for a specific execution.

ListPrivateConnections.name: ListPrivateConnections
ListPrivateConnections.documentation: Lists all Private Connections in the caller's account.

ListRecommendations.name: ListRecommendations
ListRecommendations.documentation: Lists recommendations for the specified agent space

ListServices.name: ListServices
ListServices.documentation: List a list of registered service on the account level.

ListTagsForResource.name: ListTagsForResource
ListTagsForResource.documentation: Lists tags for the specified AWS DevOps Agent resource.

SendMessage.name: SendMessage
SendMessage.documentation: Sends a chat message and streams the response for the specified agent space execution

TagResource.name: TagResource
TagResource.documentation: Adds or overwrites tags for the specified AWS DevOps Agent resource.

UntagResource.name: UntagResource
UntagResource.documentation: Removes tags from the specified AWS DevOps Agent resource.

UpdateAgentSpace.name: UpdateAgentSpace
UpdateAgentSpace.documentation: Updates the information of an existing AgentSpace.

UpdateAssociation.name: UpdateAssociation
UpdateAssociation.documentation: Partially updates the configuration of an existing service association for an AgentSpace. Present fields are fully replaced; absent fields are left unchanged. Returns 200 OK on success.

UpdateOperatorAppIdpConfig.name: UpdateOperatorAppIdpConfig
UpdateOperatorAppIdpConfig.documentation: Update the external Identity Provider configuration for the Operator App

UpdatePrivateConnectionCertificate.name: UpdatePrivateConnectionCertificate
UpdatePrivateConnectionCertificate.documentation: Updates the certificate associated with a Private Connection.

UpdateRecommendation.name: UpdateRecommendation
UpdateRecommendation.documentation: Updates an existing recommendation with new content, status, or metadata

ValidateAwsAssociations.name: ValidateAwsAssociations
ValidateAwsAssociations.documentation: Validates an aws association and set status and returns a 204 No Content response on success.

assumableRoleArn.documentation: Role ARN to be assumed by AIDevOps to operate on behalf of customer.

accountId.documentation: AWS Account Id corresponding to provided resources.

AWSConfiguration.documentation: Configuration for AWS monitor account integration, allowing AIDevOps to monitor AWS resources.

message.documentation: Detailed error message describing why access was denied.

AccessDeniedException.documentation: Access to the requested resource is denied due to insufficient permissions.

azureidentity.documentation: Azure identity details for services using Azure authentication.

AdditionalServiceDetails.documentation: Union of service-specific details for different service types.

AdditionalServiceRegistrationStep.documentation: Additional steps required to complete service registration.

locale.documentation: The locale for the AgentSpace, which determines the language used in agent responses.

createdAt.documentation: The timestamp when the resource was created.

updatedAt.documentation: The timestamp when the resource was last updated.

kmsKeyArn.documentation: The ARN of the AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) customer managed key that's used to encrypt resources.

AgentSpace.documentation: Represents a complete AgentSpace with all its properties, timestamps, encryption settings, and unique identifier.

AssistantMessageBlock.documentation: A block of content in an assistant message.

configuration.documentation: The configuration that directs how AgentSpace interacts with the given service.

AssociateServiceOutput.documentation: Output containing the newly created association and optional webhook configuration.

subscriptionId.documentation: Azure subscription ID corresponding to provided resources.

CertificateString.documentation: String type for certificate or certificate chain.

updatedAt.documentation: Timestamp when the chat was last updated

locale.documentation: The locale for the AgentSpace, which determines the language used in agent responses.

kmsKeyArn.documentation: The ARN of the AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) customer managed key that's used to encrypt resources.

clientToken.documentation: Client-provided token to ensure request idempotency. When the same token is provided in subsequent calls, the same response is returned within a 8-hour window.

tags.documentation: Tags to add to the AgentSpace at creation time.

maxResults.documentation: Maximum number of results to return in a single call.

ListAssociationsOutput.documentation: Output containing a list of service associations and pagination token.

agentSpaceId.documentation: The unique identifier for the agent space containing the tasks

filter.documentation:
Filter criteria to apply when listing tasks

Filtering restrictions:

  • Each filter field list is limited to a single value
  • Filtering by Priority and Status at the same time when not filtering by Type is not permitted
  • Timestamp filters (createdAfter, createdBefore) can be combined with other filters when not sorting by priority

limit.documentation: Maximum number of tasks to return in a single response (1-1000, default: 100)

nextToken.documentation: Token for retrieving the next page of results

sortField.documentation:
Field to sort by

Sorting restrictions:
  • Only sorting on createdAt is supported when using priority or status filters alone.
  • Sorting by priority is not supported when using Timestamp filters (createdAfter, createdBefore)

order.documentation: Sort order for the tasks based on sortField (default: DESC)

ListBacklogTasksRequest.documentation: Request structure for listing backlog tasks with filtering, sorting, and pagination support

nextToken.documentation: Token for retrieving the next page of results, if more results are available

ListBacklogTasksResponse.documentation: Response structure containing a list of backlog tasks

userId.documentation: The user identifier to list chats for. This field is deprecated and will be ignored — the service resolves user identity from the authenticated session.
userId.deprecatedMessage: userId is managed by the service and should not be provided by the caller

nextToken.documentation: Token for retrieving the next page of results

taskId.documentation: The unique identifier of the task whose executions to retrieve

RegisteredSlackServiceDetails.documentation: Details specific to a registered Slack workspace.

ResourceConfigurationArn.documentation: The ID or ARN of the Resource Configuration.

ResourceId.documentation: Generic resource identifier (allows alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores; 1-128 characters)

message.documentation: Detailed error message describing which resource was not found.

ResourceNotFoundException.documentation: The requested resource could not be found.

SchedulerState.documentation: State of Goal Schedule. Mirrors EventBridge Scheduler State

resourceConfigurationId.documentation: The ID or ARN of the resource configuration.

SendMessageContentBlockDelta.documentation: Union of possible delta payloads within a content block delta event

SendMessageContentBlockDeltaEvent.documentation: Event emitted for each incremental content delta within a content block

type.documentation: The type of content in this block

parentId.documentation: Optional parent block ID for nested content blocks (e.g. subagent tool calls)

SendMessageContentBlockStartEvent.documentation: Event emitted when a new content block starts

type.documentation: The type of content in this block

last.documentation: Whether this is the final content block in the response

SendMessageContentBlockStopEvent.documentation: Event emitted when a content block is complete

currentPage.documentation: The current page or view the user is on

lastMessage.documentation: The ID of the last message in the conversation

userActionResponse.documentation: Response to a UI prompt (not a text conversation message)

responseInProgress.documentation: Emitted while the response is being generated

summary.documentation: Emitted to provide a summary of agent actions

heartbeat.documentation: Heartbeat event sent periodically to keep the connection alive during idle periods

contentBlockStart.documentation: Emitted when a new content block starts

contentBlockDelta.documentation: Emitted for each incremental content delta within a content block

contentBlockStop.documentation: Emitted when a content block is complete

SendMessageEvents.documentation:
Event stream for chat message responses using the content block model. Events follow a lifecycle: responseCreated -> responseInProgress -> (contentBlockStart/contentBlockDelta/contentBlockStop events) -> responseCompleted|responseFailed

SendMessage always uses content block mode — legacy per-field events (outputTextDelta, functionCallArgumentsDelta, etc.) are not emitted.

executionId.documentation: The execution identifier for the chat session

userId.documentation: User identifier. This field is deprecated and will be ignored — the service resolves user identity from the authenticated session.
userId.deprecatedMessage: userId is managed by the service and should not be provided by the caller

SendMessageRequest.documentation: Request structure for sending a chat message

SendMessageResponse.documentation: Response structure for sending chat message events

SendMessageResponseCompletedEvent.documentation: Event emitted when the response completes successfully

SendMessageResponseCreatedEvent.documentation: Event emitted when the response is created

SendMessageResponseInProgressEvent.documentation: Event emitted while the response is being generated

Service.documentation: Enumeration of all supported service types, combining OAuth 3-legged, client credentials, and simple token authentication methods.

sourceAws.documentation: AWS source account configuration for monitoring resources.

ServiceConfiguration.documentation: Union of all supported service configuration types. Each service has its own specific configuration structure.

azureidentity.documentation: Azure integration with AWS Outbound Identity Federation specific service details.

ServiceDetails.documentation: Union of service-specific configuration details for service registration.

hostAddress.documentation: IP address or DNS name of the target resource.

vpcId.documentation: VPC to create the service-managed Resource Gateway in.

subnetIds.documentation: Subnets that the service-managed Resource Gateway will span.

securityGroupIds.documentation: Security groups to attach to the service-managed Resource Gateway. If not specified, a default security group is created.

ipAddressType.documentation: IP address type of the service-managed Resource Gateway.

ipv4AddressesPerEni.documentation: Number of IPv4 addresses in each ENI for the service-managed Resource Gateway.

portRanges.documentation: TCP port ranges that a consumer can use to access the resource.

ServiceName.documentation: The display name for a registered service.

authScopes.documentation: Scoped down authentication scopes for fine grained control

clientName.documentation: User friendly OAuth client name specified by end user.

clientId.documentation: OAuth client ID for authenticating with the service.

exchangeParameters.documentation: OAuth token exchange parameters for authenticating with the service.

clientSecret.documentation: OAuth client secret for authenticating with the service.

authorizationConfig.documentation: ServiceNow OAuth client credentials configuration. Use this when registering with OAuth client credentials flow.

message.documentation: Detailed error message describing which quota was exceeded.

ServiceQuotaExceededException.documentation: The request would exceed the service quota limit.

SlackChannel.documentation: Represents a Slack channel with its ID and optional name.

SlackTransmissionTarget.documentation: Defines Slack channels for different types of agent notifications.

accountId.documentation: AWS Account Id corresponding to provided resources.

assumableRoleArn.documentation: Role ARN to be assumed by AIDevOps to operate on behalf of customer.

externalId.documentation: External ID for additional security when assuming the role. Used to prevent the confused deputy problem.

SourceAwsConfiguration.documentation: Configuration for AWS source account integration. Note: passRole check on 'assumableRoleArn' is not supported.

resourceArn.documentation: The ARN of the resource to tag.

agentSpaceId.documentation: The unique identifier for the agent space containing this task

executionId.documentation: The execution ID associated with this task, if any

reference.documentation: Optional reference information linking this task to external systems

updatedAt.documentation: Timestamp when this task was last updated

primaryTaskId.documentation: The task ID of the primary investigation this task is linked to

statusReason.documentation: Explanation for why the task status was changed (e.g., linked reason)

hasLinkedTasks.documentation: Indicates if this task has other tasks linked to it

Task.documentation: Represents a backlog task with all its properties and metadata

createdAfter.documentation: Filter for tasks created after this timestamp inclusive

createdBefore.documentation: Filter for tasks created before this timestamp exclusive

primaryTaskId.documentation: Filter by primary task ID to get linked tasks

TaskFilter.documentation: Filter criteria for listing backlog tasks, supporting time range, priority, status, and type filters.

TaskStatus.documentation: Possible states of a task throughout its lifecycle

TaskType.documentation: Types of tasks that can be created in the backlog

message.documentation: Detailed error message describing the throttling condition.

ThrottlingException.documentation: The request was throttled due to too many requests. Please slow down and try again.

resourceArn.documentation: The ARN of the resource to untag.

locale.documentation: The updated locale for the AgentSpace, which determines the language used in agent responses.

UpdateAgentSpaceInput.documentation: Input for updating an existing AgentSpace's properties. All fields except agentSpaceId are optional for partial updates.

associationId.documentation: The unique identifier of the given association.

configuration.documentation: The configuration that directs how AgentSpace interacts with the given service. The entire configuration is replaced on update.

UpdateAssociationInput.documentation: Input for updating an existing service association. Present fields are fully replaced; absent fields are left unchanged.

UpdateAssociationOutput.documentation: Output containing the updated association and optional webhook configuration.

agentSpaceId.documentation: The unique identifier for the agent space containing the task

taskId.documentation: The unique identifier of the task to update

agentSpaceId.documentation: The unique identifier for the agent space containing the goal

goalId.documentation: The unique identifier of the goal to update

idpClientSecret.documentation: The OIDC client secret for the IdP application

UpdateOperatorAppIdpConfigInput.documentation: Input for updating the external Identity Provider configuration for the Operator App.

certificate.documentation: The new certificate for the Private Connection.

UpdatePrivateConnectionCertificateInput.documentation: Input for updating the certificate of a Private Connection.

resourceGatewayId.documentation: The service-managed Resource Gateway ARN. Only present for service-managed Private Connections.

hostAddress.documentation: IP address or DNS name of the target resource. Only present for service-managed Private Connections.

vpcId.documentation: VPC identifier of the service-managed Resource Gateway. Only present for service-managed Private Connections.

resourceConfigurationId.documentation: The Resource Configuration ARN. Only present for self-managed Private Connections.

certificateExpiryTime.documentation: The expiry time of the certificate associated with the Private Connection. Only present when a certificate is associated.

UpdatePrivateConnectionCertificateOutput.documentation: Output containing the updated Private Connection summary.

agentSpaceId.documentation: The unique identifier for the agent space containing the recommendation

recommendationId.documentation: The unique identifier for the recommendation to update

clientToken.documentation: A unique token that ensures idempotency of the request

UpdateRecommendationRequest.documentation: Request structure for updating an existing recommendation

UsageMetric.documentation: Represents a usage metric with its configured limit and current usage value.

toolResult.documentation: Tool execution result provided by the user.

UserMessageBlock.documentation: A block of content in a user message.

UserReference.documentation: Reference to a user in the system

ValidateAwsAssociationsOutput.documentation: Empty Output for successful validating an aws association

fieldList.documentation: A list of specific failures encountered while validating the input. A member can appear in this list more than once if it failed to satisfy multiple constraints.

ValidationException.documentation: A standard error for input validation failures. This should be thrown by services when a member of the input structure falls outside of the modeled or documented constraints.

path.documentation: A JSONPointer expression to the structure member whose value failed to satisfy the modeled constraints.

message.documentation: A detailed description of the validation failure.

ValidationExceptionField.documentation: Describes one specific validation failure for an input member.

ValidationStatus.documentation: Represents the validation state of an association.

WebIdentityTokenAudienceList.documentation: List of audiences for Web Identity Token.

Webhook.documentation: Represents a complete Webhook with all its properties, and unique identifier.

documentation:
AWS DevOps Agent is a frontier agent that resolves and proactively prevents incidents, continuously improving reliability and performance. AWS DevOps Agent investigates incidents and identifies operational improvements as an experienced DevOps engineer.

The agent works by:

  • Learning your resources and their relationships.
  • Working with your observability tools, runbooks, code repositories, and CI/CD pipelines.
  • Correlating telemetry, code, and deployment data to understand relationships between your application resources.
  • Supporting applications in multicloud and hybrid environments.
